name: Newsletter signup
description: Newsletter signup is a section with a form which allows users to subscribe to a newsletter for updates. There is a separated [Newsletter signup block](/sites/construct/newsletter-signup-block), which is for putting all the same content to the block item and allow to place it within a different section. The Newsletter signup pattern is a strategic engagement touchpoint, most commonly placed after meaningful content, where it acts as a lightweight conversion step to keep users connected beyond their current visit.
usage:
Use newsletter signup to capture ongoing interest from users who have engaged with content but are not ready to take a primary action. Common scenarios include users who have explored a product or feature without committing; or scrolled through a landing page without converting.

### **Layout**

The pattern supports two column layout variants. Both contain the same content — only the column proportions change. Choose the variant that best aligns with the column layout of the sections adjacent to the newsletter signup on the page.

**50/50 layout:** The title and form content share equal column widths. Use when balancing messaging and form content more evenly, or when aligning with surrounding sections that use a 50/50 split.

![image.png](images/d2725753-ba1d-429b-b28b-23e6b2df84d0.png)

**25/75 layout:** The title occupies a narrow left column and the form content spans a wider right column. Use when the signup form is the primary focus of the section.

![image.png](images/59eceac1-bbb5-4a40-8363-07d65760d4ab.png)

### Composition

Newsletter signup pattern is not intended to be embedded within other patterns such as cards, lists, or multi-column layouts.

Within the section, use [Newsletter signup block](/sites/construct/newsletter-signup-block) instead. This block can also exist as a reusable unit used across different contexts. In article templates, for example, it is included by default and positioned at the top of the page as part of the template structure rather than a contextual placement decision.

In general usage, the pattern should appear as a distinct section in the page flow with clear separation from surrounding content.

### When to use

*   The user has consumed meaningful content and may want to stay connected
*   There is no clear primary conversion action on the page
*   The goal is to maintain a low-commitment relationship with an interested but undecided user

### When not to use

*   The placement is too early in the page. Placing newsletter signup before any value has been communicated gives users no reason to subscribe. Establish value first with a Hero section or introductory content, then introduce the signup as a follow-up action.
*   When the newsletter signup needs to appear within another section or pattern, use the [Newsletter signup block](/sites/construct/newsletter-signup-block) instead of the full pattern

table.name: Rule
table.description: The rule component indicates the beginning of a new section.

table.name: Heading
table.description: The section heading communicates the primary subject or theme of the section.

table.name: Body
table.description: Body text providing context for the signup. One or more paragraphs.

table.name: Label + input
table.description: A single-line text [Input](wiki-ref://grid-ymtdOF6n4R/i-9t3OC1fOBH) for the user's email address, paired with a visible label

table.name: Checkbox + label
table.description: A [Checkbox](wiki-ref://grid-ymtdOF6n4R/i-hES7I02ImG) paired with a label confirming the user's consent to receive communications.

table.name: Addendum
table.description: Additional content to include in the form, such as a disclaimer or supplementary information. Paragraph-style content.

table.name: Button
table.description: A single [Button](wiki-ref://grid-ymtdOF6n4R/i-nsWL1xi6H5) that submits the form.

properties.name: Section rule
properties.description: The rule component indicates the beginning of a new pattern
properties.constraint: Only use the default style for the section-level rule

properties.name: Body
properties.description: Paragraph-style content. One or more paragraphs.

properties.name: Input label
properties.description: Label for the email input field.

properties.name: Addendum
properties.description: Paragraph-style content to include in the form, such as a disclaimer or supplementary information.

properties.name: Button
properties.description: The CTA using the default button style. It is used for most actions that are positive but not primary, and may serve as the main CTA in non-hero sections.

properties.name: Bottom padding
properties.description: The type of padding applied to the bottom of the pattern

properties.name: State
properties.description: The state the input is currently in.

properties.name: Input validation type
properties.description: If the input is being validated what type of input validation it is.

properties.name: Input validation text
properties.description: If the input is being validated the outcome / reason of the input validation

properties.name: Size
properties.description: The vertical density of the input element

properties.name: Text
properties.description: The text that is in the input field.

properties.name: Placeholder
properties.description: The placeholder text that is being shown if there has not been any text that has been entered yet.

properties.name: Checked
properties.description: In which checked state the checkbox currently is in.

properties.name: State
properties.description: The state the checkbox is currently in. Note that in the checked and child checked state there is no hover effect.

exceptionFromRules.rule: Section titles can only have one of two styles: H2 or Heading muted
